title: China stays hungry for iron ore as Simandou ramps-up
section_path: China stays hungry for iron ore as Simandou ramps-up
section_path_text: China stays hungry for iron ore as Simandou ramps-up
summary:
Main
China continues to fill its iron ore stocks as Simandou export starts to climb, and the wet season in Brazil limits export growth
Image reference: unnamed (2).png | assets/2026-03-05_china-stays-hungry-for-iron-ore-as-simandou-ramps-up_img_unnamed28229_9df11c27ca63.png
Key Takeaways
● Iron ore flows in January and February 2026 were up 8% y/y. January iron ore flows to China rose 10% to 105mt. In February, flows increased by 7%.

children.title: Main

Key Takeaways
● Iron ore flows in January and February 2026 were up 8% y/y. January iron ore flows to China rose 10% to 105mt.

children.title: Guinea and Brazil will shape the Atlantic iron ore market
section_path: Guinea and Brazil will shape the Atlantic iron ore market
children.section_path_text: Guinea and Brazil will shape the Atlantic iron ore market
children.summary: The continued ramp-up of Simandou remains the biggest factor in iron ore flows in 2026. Over the upcoming month, we expect Brazilian iron ore exports to remain under pressure due to the rains, while Guinea exports rise.
